tips.title: First Layer Adhesion
tips.text: Clean your bed with IPA before every print. Oils from fingers are the #1 cause of first-layer adhesion failures.

tips.title: Stringing Fix
tips.text: Reduce retraction to 0.8-1.2mm for direct drive, 4-6mm for Bowden. Also try lowering nozzle temp by 5C.

tips.title: PLA Storage
tips.text: Store PLA in airtight containers with silica gel. Moisture causes popping, stringing, and weak adhesion.

tips.title: PETG Bed Temp
tips.text: PETG loves 75-85C bed temp. Use glue stick as release agent - PETG can bond TOO well to glass.

tips.title: Calibration Cube
tips.text: Print a 20mm cal cube regularly. Measure with calipers - if not 20.0mm, adjust steps/mm.

tips.title: Support Removal
tips.text: Set support Z-distance to 0.2mm for easy removal. Tree supports often give cleaner results.

tips.title: Speed vs Quality
tips.text: For detail: 30-40mm/s. For functional: 80-120mm/s. Input shaper lets you push speed without ringing.

tips.title: Nozzle Maintenance
tips.text: Cold pull weekly: heat to 200C, push filament, cool to 90C, pull. The blob brings out debris.

tips.title: Layer Height Rule
tips.text: Max layer height = 75% of nozzle diameter. For 0.4mm nozzle, thats 0.3mm max.

tips.title: Bridging Tips
tips.text: Better bridges: fan 100%, bridge flow 90-95%, bridge speed 15-20mm/s.

tips.title: TPU Printing
tips.text: Print TPU at 20-30mm/s, no retraction on Bowden. Direct drive handles TPU much better.

tips.title: Enclosure Benefits
tips.text: An enclosure stabilizes ambient temp for all materials, reducing warping. Even a cardboard box helps!

tips.title: Ironing for Smooth Tops
tips.text: Enable Ironing: flow 10-15%, speed 20mm/s. Great for name plates and flat-top models.

tips.title: Bed Leveling Frequency
tips.text: Even with ABL, re-run mesh probe every few weeks. Beds shift, especially on moved printers.

tips.title: Filament Runout
tips.text: No runout sensor? Weigh your spool. A nearly-empty 1kg spool with holder weighs about 250g.
